title: Smoky Roasted Jamaican Tofu Casserole
description: A comforting, fermentedflavored tofu casserole with smoky spices, roasted vegetables, and a gooey coconut milk topping, perfect for a diabeticfriendly dinner.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a bowl, whisk tofu cubes with soy sauce, tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, allspice, and minced garlic until well coated.
Heat coconut oil in a skillet over medium heat; sauté coated tofu until lightly browned, about 5 minutes, then transfer to the prepared baking sheet.
Scatter diced bell pepper and shredded cabbage over the tofu on the sheet.
Drizzle olive oil over the vegetables, toss to coat, and season with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Roast in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, stirring halfway, until vegetables are tender and edges are crisp.
While roasting, gently warm the coconut milk in a small saucepan, adding a pinch of salt, then set aside.
After roasting, remove the casserole from the oven; spoon warm coconut milk over the top and place back under the broiler for 3–5 minutes until golden and bubbly.
Let cool for 2 minutes, then garnish with fresh thyme leaves if desired.
Serve hot, paired with a side of quinoa or brown rice for a complete meal.
